utilities.note: electric ~$54 + internet ~$43; cool highland climate means no A/C

groceries.note: groceries $200–$350; mix of local markets and supermarkets; USD is local currency

diningOut.note: local fondas + expat cafes; restaurant scene growing

transit.note: car strongly recommended; $100–$200 with car; taxis otherwise ~$80

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; limited formal coworking; cafes fill the gap

simData.note: Cable & Wireless/Claro 30-day plan; USD-based so no FX issues

misc.note: gym, toiletries, entertainment, hiking gear, nature activities, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base.

evidence.quote: Everyday life in Boquete is moderately priced at about $1,360 per month, rent included
rents for a 1-bedroom flat in Boquete range from $640 to $764, depending on location and condition. In Boquete you can rent a whole house for US$600 to US$1,200, or a one-bedroom for US$500 to US$800

evidence.claim: electric ~$54, internet ~$43/month; no A/C needed in highlands
evidence.quote: Electricity, water, cooling, heating, and garbage services: around $54 per month; High-speed internet (60 Mbps or more, unlimited data): around $43 per month
Groceries (mix of local markets + supermarket) ($200–$350), transport (car ownership strongly recommended) ($100–$200)
